rationale: The signal set is dominated by Google autocomplete suggestions (95 entries) around general care topics (food, enclosure, lifespan, diet, breeding basics), morph identification, and pricing. YouTube content shows heavy existing coverage of morph guides (multiple "complete guides," "comprehensive guides"), cappuccino genetics debates (already queued as super-cappuccino-breeding-risks and lilly-white-identification-breeding), and basic breeding advice. The breeder blog signal is purely promotional (sales announcement). No forum threads, Bluesky posts, or Google Trends spikes to signal emerging confusion or controversy. The autocomplete data itself is too generic and diffuse—it shows demand for beginner-level content (care, diet, enclosure) which we've already published, and morph-specific searches which duplicate either our existing slug (cappuccino-crested-gecko-genetics, how-to-identify-crested-gecko-morphs) or already-queued topics (super-cappuccino-breeding-risks, lilly-white-identification-breeding, axanthic-breeding-strategy). YouTube titles like "WARNING, NEVER BREED THESE CRESTED GECKOS TOGETHER" and "Why So Much Hate for Cappuccino Geckos? The Truth No One Wants to Say…" suggest strong community opinion, but without access to the actual video content or forum discussion threads showing specific misconceptions, we cannot ground a fact-checked post. The signal lacks the specificity needed to identify a concrete breeding outcome, genetics correction, or breeder-to-breeder angle that we can verify against our morph guide and genetics resources.
